merger_name: Australian Venue Co – assets of Boatbuilders Yard and General Assembly, Melbourne

targets.name: DOG AT THE BRIDGE PTY LTD

merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Ltd (**AVC**), a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of substantially all of the assets of two licensed hospitality businesses in Victoria, being: (i) the business trading as The Boatbuilders Yard (**BBY Business**); and (ii) the business trading as General Assembly (**GA Business**), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (together, the **Acquisition**).

The acquirer, AVC, operates over 200 licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as some bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and are mostly located in inner-city and metropolitan areas, offering a range of products and services including food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.

The BBY Business and the GA Business are standalone pub sites. The BBY Business and the GA Business are currently owned and operated by Shed 4 (Vic) Pty Ltd and Dog At The Bridge Pty Ltd respectively (**Vendors**).

The Vendors are associated with the Riverland Group, a retail hospitality group that operates licensed venues across Victoria (primarily in Melbourne, as well as one business in South Australia) comprising primarily of pubs, bars and licensed restaurants.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1)

determination_table_content.details:
AVC Operations Pty Ltd (AVC), a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co.
Holdings Limited, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of substantially all of the assets of two
licensed hospitality businesses in Victoria, being: (i) the business
trading as The Boatbuilders Yard (BBY Business) and (ii) the
business trading as The General Assembly (GA Business), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (together, the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AVC, operates over 200 licensed venues throughout
Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as some bars,
clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually
branded and are mostly located in inner-city and metropolitan areas,
offering a range of products and services including food, alcoholic
and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and
gaming facilities.
The BBY Business and the GA Business are standalone pub sites.
The BBY Business and the GA Business are currently owned and
operated by Shed 4 (Vic) Pty Ltd and Dog At The Bridge Pty Ltd
respectively (Vendors).
The Vendors are associated with the Riverland Group, a retail
hospitality group that operates licensed venues across Victoria
(primarily in Melbourne, as well as one business in South Australia)
comprising primarily of pubs, bars and licensed restaurants.
The parties both supply retail hospitality services in metropolitan
Melbourne.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the parties combined share, based on number of premises,
in the supply of dining, functions and on-premises liquor
services at pub-style venues in the local area surrounding
the BBY Business and GA Business is low
b. there are alternative pub-style venues which supply dining,
functions and liquor for on-site consumption in the local
area surrounding the BBY Business and GA Business and in
the broader Melbourne metropolitan area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
